crash_records.narrative: Unit 2 is an electric scooter that does not contain a VIN number or any identifying serial numbers.  Unit 2 was riden by the driver of that unit into the roadway of N Valley Mills Dr in the 1000 BLK.  Unit 2 entered the roadway from a private driveway and failed to yield the right of way to traffic that was on the roadway.  Unit 2 also did not cross in a designated crosswalk or any other protected location.  Unit 2's choice to cross at night from a private drive contributed to unit 2 causing the crash where unit 1, which was southbound in the 1000 BLK of N Valley Mills Drive hit unit 2.  Unit 1 was unable to stop when unit 2 exited the private drive in front of the driver in a manner that left unit 1 with no evasive actions available to them other than striking unit 2 with the FR of unit 1. Unit 2's rider was transported to hillcrest for medical treatment, however was not cited at fault since due to his injuries there was not opportunity or ability to interview him.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was traveling north bound on 7400 block of Weybridge turning westbound onto the 7400 block of Brandyridge. Unit 2 was a motorized two wheel bicycle traveling southbound from the 7500 block of Weybridge. Unit 1 collided into Unit 2 as Unit 1 attempted to turn onto the 7400 block of Brandyridge. Upon my arrival EMS and cover officers were already at location, and EMS was conducting CPR on Unit 2 operator. Cover officer was standing with Unit 1 driver. Scene was then secure, Roads were then shut down from oncoming traffic for further investigation. I then contacted Unit 1 driver. Unit 1 driver stated she was traveling northbound on the 7400 block of Weybridge and as she attempted to turn westbound onto Brandyridge she then hit Unit 2. Unit 1 driver stated she did not see Unit 2 as she was turning. Unit 1 driver stated she saw Unit 2 as the impact happened. Unit 1 driver stated she immediately moved Unit 1 after the impact to prevent further accidents at location. I then conducted HGN on Unit 1 Driver and was unable to see any clues coming from Unit 1 driver. It appeared that Unit 2 did not have a light attached to its motorized bicycle.I then contacted TID Detective Martinez #2407 who advised me to make sure the scene is secure, contact CSI and hold all units at their location unit TID makes location. CSI J. Imler #9423 was contacted for photographs. Unit 2 Driver was then transported to University Hospital by M41 where he was then pronounced deceased at 0047 Hours on 07/16/2021. Unit 2 was transported to Growdon Pound by Mission Wrecker TSR: A249548. Unit 2 driver personal property was taken to the property room by cover Officer C. KYNE #0392.On 10/15/2021 I received the toxicology report that showed Unit 2 driver to have a BAC of .19

crash_records.narrative: Unit #1 was traveling S/B 2300 S Carter St and had a solid green light to cross over 2400 E Arkansas Ln. Unit #2 was crossing over the intersection from a diagonal line (southwest corner to northeast corner). Unit #1 struck Unit #2 in the intersection. Driver of Unit #2 could not provide any details regarding the accident and had been possibly drinking prior to accident.  Unit #2 was a Red S-650 Schwinn Scooter (Electric) Serial #ACA08A21359. crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 pulled into the private drive to drop off construction supplies and drove down the wrong way on a one way street. Unit 1 went north sound in the south bound one way. On that street was a cross walk and a stop sign for the one way traffic coming south bound on the street.Unit 2 was on his eletric riding scooter coming in from the park. Unit 2 then proceeded into the crosswalk as Unit 1 was driving in the crosswalk. Unit 2 and 1 hit behind the drivers door of the truck on the flat bed of the truck. It looks like the rear tires of the truck might have ran over the small front bumper of the cart. Unit 2 was complaing of sholder pain and EMS was called to the scene. Unit 2 was transported to the hospital. The eletric scooter was taken by management and placed in the lobby until he got back from the hosptial. I was able to see the video of the incident and took the video and placed it into evidence.

UNIT 2 HAD A BLINKING YELLOW ARROW WHEN IT PROCEEDED THROUGH THE INTERSECTION. UNIT 1 WAS TRAVELING SOUTH BOUND ALONG 600 STUDEWOOD ST AND HAD A GREEN LIGHT WHEN IT STRUCK UNIT 2 IN THE INTERSECTION. UNIT 1 HAD THE RIGHT OF WAY. UNIT 1 IS AN ELECTRIC SCOOTER ( NOT A MOPED) ON WHICH YOU STAND ON. UNIT 1 IS NOT A MOTOR VEHICLE. UNIT 1 DID NOT HAVE A HEADLAMP ON. crash_records.narrative: I received a police radio dispatch to the Heritage Apartments, 3102 4th St, in reference to a hit and run accident. U2 was riding a Lime scooter on the sidewalk and began crossing the parking lot exit of the Heritage apartments. U1 was travelling southbound in the parking lot of the Heritage Apartments. U1 was turning westbound onto the 3100 block of 4th St. U1 failed to yield the right of way and accelerated while U2 was crossing the sidewalk. U1 then struck the back end of the Lime Scooter and ran over U2's foot. U1 then fled the scene headed westbound in the 3100 block of 4th St at a high rate of speed. U2 was thrown off of the scooter. Witnesses helped U2 get to a safe place and out of the roadway. I arrived on scene and observed a Lime Scooter laying in the northernmost eastbound lane of the 3100 block of 4th St. I made contact with U2 and he advised that U1 appeared to slow down to allow U2 to pass the exit of the parking lot. U1 then accelerated unexpectedly. U2 did not think he was hit on purpose. U2 provided a partial Texas license plate number of 0579. See report number 21-45649 for further.WitnessMartinez, Duvan (1/18/89) 806-445-8411Herring, Jerry (12/2/59) 806-773-8419Both witnesses are maintenance workers at the Heritage Apartments. They advised that they were working nearby and heard U2 scream. Both witnesses looked and saw a new model black Dodge Durango speeding away and U2 laying on the ground.

crash_records.narrative: On the above date and time, I was dispatched to Karen Lane and Cascade Place for a traffic crash involving a motor vehicle (Unit 1), and a male operating a battery operated scooter (Unit 2).  This morning had very dense and low fog, wet roadways and it made it very difficult conditions to see.  Upon arrival, I made contact with the driver of Unit 1.  Unit 1 stated that she was not injured and declined EMS.  For this intersection, it is a two way stop sign on Cascade Place, Karen Lane has the right of way traffic.  Unit 1 reports that she was traveling west on Karen Lane when she came to the intersection of Cascade Place.  Unit 2 was traveling south on Cascade Place.  Unit 1 reports that Unit 2 ran the stop sign and crashed into her vehicle causing damages.  Unit 1 reports that she tried to help Unit 2 by calling 911.  Unit 1 stated that Unit 2 said that he was not okay and did not want to do anything about it.  Unit 2 walked several yards up where EMS was able to make contact with him.  EMS checked him and stated that that his injuries were not life threatening.  Unit 2 had a hurt arm and bumped his head.  Unit 2 was transported to SAMC for his possible injury.  Unit 2 reported that he thought he had a green light and did not see Unit 1 coming when he entered the intersection.  There was a witness who stopped (W1 *see key card for service for W1 information.)  W1 stated that he was traveling east on Karen and he saw Unit 2 run the stop sign and crash into Unit 1's vehicle causing damages to both.  He also stated that Unit 2 was also trying to call 911 for help on his phone.  I contacted Sgt. Smith, G. #3050 and Det. Valenzuela, R. D. #2189 with Traffic Investigations and advised them of the events.  Both Units exchanged information and was provided with this case number.  Officer O'Neal #1032 took Unit 2's scooter and placed it in the property room under barcode #990001653591.  Coban/BWC.

crash_records.narrative: I OFFICER DODSON #7844 RESPONDED TO A CRASH HOT SHOT AT THE 1200 BLOCK OF NEWFIELD LANE. THE CALL TEXT ADVISED THAT THERE WAS A AN AUTO-PED ACCIDENT AT THIS LOCATION.  I WAS NOT THE FIRST TO ARRIVE ON SCENE NOR WAS I THE FIRST INVESTIGATING OFFICER.  I DID SPEAK WITH OFFICERS ON SCENE AND I DID INVESTIGATE THE PHYSICAL EVIDENCE LEFT BEHIND AT THE SCENE OF THE CRASH. THE ACCIDENT WAS BETWEEN UNIT 1, A WHITE CHEVY, AND UNIT 2 A PEDESTRIAN ON A RIDE SHARE SCOOTER. ACCORDING TO WITNESSES ON SCENE UNIT 1 LEFT MOPAC AT THE ENFIELD/NEWFIELD EXIT, 1200 BLOCK OF NEWFIELD LANE.  ONCE ON NEWFIELD UNIT 1 STAYED IN THE FAR LEFT LANE AS IT CONTINUED INTO THE 1300 BLOCK OF NEWFIELD.  UNIT 2 WAS IN THE FAR LEFT LANE AND RIDING AGAINST TRAFFIC.   THE UNITS COLLIDED WITH EACH OTHER IN ABOUT THE 1300 BLOCK OF NEWFIELD LN. UNIT 2 STRUCK UNIT ONE AT ABOUT WHERE THE FRONT LEFT HEADLIGHT IS. THE PEDESTRIAN HIT THE DRIVER SIDE WINDSHIELD AND MADE PARTIAL ENTRY BEFORE BEING FLUNG OFF IN THE GRASSY AREA ALONG THE WEST SIDE OF THE ROAD WAY.  THE SCOOTER WAS THEN DRUG UNDERNEATH UNIT 1 AND UNIT 1 CONTINUED ON UNTIL IT CAME TO A STOP AT THE INTERSECTION OF ENFIELD RD AND NEWFIELD LANE.     THERE WERE DEEP GOUGES IN THE ROAD WAY STARTING FROM THE AREA OF THE INTIAL IMPACT ALL THE WAY UNTIL WHERE THE UNIT 1 HAD COME TO A STOP.   THE PEDESTRIAN ULTIMATELY SUCCUMBED TO HIS INJURIES ON SCENE AND THE DRIVER OF UNIT 2 WAS ARRESTED FOR DRIVIING WHILE INTOXICATED. BAC for Driver #2 was .20 per TCME's report.

crash_records.narrative: I was dispatched to listed location for a Motor Vehicle/Pedestrian Accident. Upon arrival I contacted Unit 1 driver who advised he was not injured. I then contacted P1 who also advised he was not injured. Unit 1 driver advised he was traveling EB on W Quincy street and had a green signal light to proceed through the intersection of San Pedro Ave. Unit 1 driver was then knocked off of his motorcycle when P1 and P2, who were on a Motorized Scooter (Byrd #Y67CK) failed to stop at the intersection red light while going NB on San Pedro Ave. Unit 1 had minor scrapes on the left side of motorcycle due to it falling on the street.I contacted P1 who advised he was operating a motorized scooter traveling NB on San Pedro and did not know how to stop the scooter. P1 advised P2 was riding on the back of his scooter. P1 stated he entered the W Quincy intersection and collided with the right side of Unit 1. P1 advised his girlfriend P2 left the scene and she was uninjured. I did not observed any outstanding damages to motorized scooter. Unit 1 drivers friend provided Go Pro video of accident which showed P1 and P2 on a motorized scooter failing to stop at a red light while traveling NB in the NB lanes of San Pedro Ave. P1 and P2 collided with Unit 1 driver as he was crossing the intersection traveling EB on W Quincy St.Unit 1 driver and P1 were provided with case numbers for the incident. Unit 1 driver was found to not have a Drivers license and No insurance and was issued Traffic Citation # M522463.Unit 1 and Unit 1 driver left location via family member.

crash_records.narrative: VEH 1 TURNING TO WESTBOUND CESAR CHAVEZ FROM SOUTHBOUND RED RIVER. VEH 1 STRUCK PEDESTRIAN ON ELECTRIC SCOOTER IN CROSSWALK. VEH 1 DRIVER DESCRIBED NOT SEEING THE SCOOTERIST. OPERATOR OF THE SCOOTER DESCRIBED HAVING THE RIGHT OF WAY.

Unit 1 was traveling southbound S Buckner Blvd (SL 12) in the right lane of travel approaching the intersection for Cordell Dr. Unit 2, which was towing Unit 3, was traveling northbound S Buckner Blvd (SL 12) in the left turn lane to go westbound Cordell Dr. Unit 4, a motorized personal mobility scooter , was northbound S Buckner Blvd, crossing in an unmarked crosswalk, across Cordell Dr. Unit 5 was stopped on Cordell Dr at the stop sign waiting for Unit 4 to cross. Unit 1 was speeding over the limit and collided front distributed with Unit 3 right back quarter as it was making the left turn. Unit 3 collided left back quarter with Unit 4, trapping the operator underneath the trailer, while sliding southwest. Unit 3 then collided left back quarter with Unit 5 pushing it backwards while in a clockwise rotation. The operator of Unit 4 was transported to Baylor Hospital where he was pronounced deceased by medical staff at 10:39PM. Dallas County Medical Examiner's Office is using case number 22-09745. This is Traffic Fatality #59 for the City of Dallas in 2022. See related report number 085854-2022. This scene was forensically 3D scanned by the Dallas Police Traffic Unit and a Detective from the Vehicle Crimes Unit responded.  Driver of Unit 1 was driving under the influence of alcohol and drugs and this report is incomplete pending toxicology results for Driver of Unit 1. All body camera video and photos were uploaded to Evidence.com under incident number 22-0920387. On 5/18/2022 the air bag control module was downloaded after obtaining a search warrant and the following speeds were recorded for Unit 1: 5 seconds prior to impact Unit 1 was traveling 85 miles per hour, 3.3 seconds prior 88 miles per hour and just prior to impact 52 miles per hour. Unit 1's speed in a posted 40 mile per hour zone denied the driver of Unit 2 the ability to make a left turn. Drivers of Units 1, 2  and 3 were all driving without a Texas Driver Licenses.                                                                    ***** The Vehicle Crimes Unit Detective assigned to this fatality crash, supplemented this report to include the toxicology results for Unit 1 Driver. Unit 1 Driver blood results were positive alcohol and drugs. Alcohol: ethanol 0.083 g/100mL. Drugs: tetrahydrocannabinol 4.7 ng/mL and carboxytetrahydrocannabinol 41.9 ng/mL. The VCU Detective also corrected Unit 1 Owner name to be consistent with the vehicle registration. This report is now complete. *****

crash_records.narrative: In this incident, unit 1 was the victim and unit 2 was the unit at fault, this is due to the CR100 requiring unit 1 to be a motor vehicle. Unit 1 was traveling north bound on the feeder road of the South Fwy N approaching the intersection with a green light. Unit 2 was traveling east bound on the sidewalk north of Broadway St. Unit 2 failed to yield the right of way and crossed the feeder road from the sidewalk without stopping. Causing unit 1 to strike unit 2 as they were going through the intersection with a green light. Unit 2 was operating an electric powered scooter on a roadway that was over 30 MPH. The operator of unit 2 was not ejected, but did fall off of his electric scooter. Fire & EMS made location to evaluate the operator of unit 1, he refused treatment on scene.

crash_records.narrative: I was on patrol assigned to the downtown bike patrol unit. I was at the intersection of TRAVIS and BROADWAY and I heard the screeching of tires then a loud impact. I then observed UNIT 2 going over the hood of UNIT 1 then UNIT 2 rolled onto the street. I approached UNIT 2 and UNIT 1 as UNIT 2 was standing up. UNIT 2 stated he was not hurt or injured. UNIT 2 was on a scooter traveling EB on PEACOCK ALLEY and was proceeding onto BROADWAY. PEACOCK ALLEY and BROADWAY is an uncontrolled intersection and traffic on BROADWAY has right of way on both northbound and southbound directions. UNIT 1 was traveling SB on BROADWAY and was unable to react as UNIT 2 was proceeding from PEACOCK ALLEY traveling the wrong way on a one-way onto Broadway without yielding right of way. UNIT 1 engaged on the brakes but was unable to stop in time causing UNIT 1 to strike UNIT 2. UNIT 1 Driver did not sustain any injuries. UNIT 1 sustained minor damage to the center bottom of the front bumper. EMS was refused by both UNIT 1 and UNIT 2. UNIT 2 scooter sustained minor scratches. UNIT 1 DRIVER was a valet employee of the listed company who was driving UNIT 1 to their owner at a hotel nearby. UNIT 1 and UNIT 2 were operable. Both parties of UNIT 1 and UNIT 2 were given case number regarding incident.

crash_records.narrative: The 9700 block of Dyer St is a three lane local roadway with an unprotected median, that runs both North and South bound. It also intersects at the 5500 block of Manilla Dr. The driver of Unit 2 was driving north bound on the 9700 block of Dyer St on the far right lane, approaching the listed intersection. The driver of Unit 2 had the green light and proceeded to cross the intersection, when a pedestrian on a ""Scooter"" entered the lane of travel and was hit by Unit 2. Unit 2 only had the front right passenger rear-view mirror taken off. The driver of Unit 2 observed that the pedestrian was assessed by Fire Medical Services (FMS) though the pedestrian refused to provide his information. No injuries were reported. See WebRMS report for further details.

Unit 1 was traveling south in the outside lane of the 500 block of S. Denton Tap Road. Unit 1 turned right (west) onto the 100 block of W. Bethel Road.  Unit 2 was a GoTrax electric scooter. The rider of Unit 2 was crossing W. Bethel Road (southbound) in the crosswalk and had a ""walk"" indicator light from the traffic signal. The front right of Unit 1 struck the left side of Unit 2 as Unit 2 was in the crosswalk.  The driver of Unit 1 said he was ""in a rush"" making his last pizza delivery of the night. He turned and did not see Unit 2 because the rider was wearing dark clothing. The driver of Unit 1 denied being on his phone.   Ofc. observed the scooter's kickstand was broken.  According to the rider of Unit 2, one of the wheels is bent and the scooter is no longer operational.  Ofc. did not see any damage to Unit 1. The rider of Unit 2 was checked by medics but he only had a scratch on his left hand/forearm.Carolyn Palmer witnessed the collision (915-240-9568). She was stopped at the red light on S. Denton Tap Road at W. Bethel Road in the middle lane. Southbound traffic received a green light. She observed Unit 1 approach from behind in the outside lane and turn directly into Unit 2. Palmer believes the driver of Unit 1 was using his phone because she saw him looking down and observed light coming from a phone.  She saw that Unit 2 was in the crosswalk with a ""walk"" indicator light. She tried to honk at Unit 1 to get his attention but he did not stop.

crash_records.narrative: V1 WAS TRAVELING EAST BOUND ON W 3RD ST WITH THE INTENTION OF TURNING RIGHT ONTO SOUTH BOUND GUADALUPE ST. AN E-SCOOTER RIDER WAS TRAVELING EAST BOUND ON W 3RD ST IN THE MARKED BICYCLE LANE WITH THE INTENT OF PROCEEDING STRAIGHT THROUGH THE INTERSECTION . THE INTERSECTION HAS BOTH OPERATIONAL TRAFFIC CONTROL DEVICES FOR VEHICLES, BICYCLES, AND PEDESTRIANS THAT ALL DISPLAYED GREEN OR WALK SIGNALS. AS THE E-SCOOTER RIDER PROCEEDED THROUGH THE INTERSECTION V1 FAILED TO YIELD RIGHT OF WAY AS HE TURNED RIGHT RESULTING IN A COLLISION. THE FRONT RIGHT BUMPER OF V1 COLLIDED WITH THE MAIN SHAFT OF THE E-SCOOTER RESULTING IN THE E-SCOOTER RIDER FALLING TO THE ROADWAY. THE E-SCOOTER RIDER WAS TRANSPORTED TO DELL SETON MCUT ER VIA EMS FOR MINOR INJURIES. THE DRIVER OF V1 REMAINED ON SCENE AND WAS CITED FOR FTYROW-PED.

UNIT 2'S  PARENTS (MOM-STEPHANIE SHOKRIAN 08/01/1984 (210)872-2691, STEP DAD- JEFFREY SHOKRIAN 04/22/1979) MADE SCENE AND REFUSE ACADIAN TRANSPORTATION DUE TO HAVING MINOR INJURIES / NO LIFE THREATENING INJURIES. AFTER DEPARTURE THE PARENTS OF THE RIDER OF UNIT 2 LATER TRANSPORTED HIM ON THEIR OWN.

crash_records.narrative: On Thursday, September 22, 2022 at approximately 1356 hours, I Officer I. De La Garza (304), was dispatched to The University of Texas-Rio Grande Valley (UTRGV) main entrance located at 1201 West University Drive Edinburg, Texas in reference to a traffic collision.Upon arrival, contact was made with a group of students who were standing off the roadway and on the sidewalk. UTRGV student, Jerry Lee Gonzalez Jr. (DOB 09-19-2001) stated he was riding his electric scooter north across the crosswalk when he collided with the other vehicle. Gonzalez stated that his left foot had pain from the accident. I advised Gonzalez that an ambulance was on its way so he could be evaluated. UTRGV Public Safety Officer A. Rodriguez (401) positioned his patrol unit so Gonzalez could sit on top of the tailgate and keep his foot off the ground. Moments later, Medcare EMS unit 174 arrived and attended to Gonzalez. Gonzalez made the decision to get transported to Edinburg Regional Hospital so he could get further evaluated. Gonzalez' scooter was picked up and turned in to the police department for safe keeping during his time at the hospital. Gonzalez was advised he could pick up his scooter at any time. The scooter did not receive any damage from the accident. I then made contact with student Enedelia Ramirez (DOB 06-03-2002) who was standing by on the sidewalk. Ramirez drove around and left her vehicle in lot E3 so it could be out of the way of traffic. Ramirez was driving her 2018 white Buick Encore bearing Texas license plate KVW8480. Ramirez stated she was at the stop sign waiting for traffic to clear and then was struck by Gonzalez shortly after she started to drive. Gonzalez stated she did not see Ramirez before she began to drive away. Gonzalez stated there was no damage to her vehicle. Parking lot camera PE3-005 was reviewed from 1354 to 1356 hours. The footage showed that Gonzalez was riding his electric scooter north on the side walk off West University Drive. Gonzalez then drove off the sidewalk and onto the crosswalk of the service drive at the main entrance to UTRGV. Gonzalez did not yield or stop prior to entering the crosswalk. Ramirez was stationary at the stop sign facing east waiting for traffic to clear. Ramirez then began to accelerate her vehicle once traffic cleared and was struck by Gonzalez shortly after. Ramirez struck Gonzalez' vehicle at the right front quarter panel. The video footage showed Ramirez accelerating her vehicle prior to Gonzalez entering the crosswalk. End of narrative.

crash_records.narrative: I was dispatched to the location for the report of a cash that occurred between a pedestrian and a motor vehicle at a different location. Hospital staff called police after U2 came in with his mother and gave details of this incident. U2 was laying in his hospital bed when I made contact with him. U2 stated he was in the parking lot of the listed location ridding his electric scooter going W/B when he tried to make a right turn to avoid U1 which was traveling E/B straight towards him. U2 stated he could not get out of the way fast enough which resulted in U1 hitting him head on. U2 stated he was knocked off his electric scooter and rolled over the entire top of U1 until he fell to the ground. U2 stated U1 stopped to see if U2 was ok, and gave U2 a ride to his U2's house. U2 did not get U1's entire information only a first name but was able to provide me the license plate of U1. U2 stated that U1 admitted to be texting while driving at the time of the crash. U2's mother brought U2 to the listed location to have U2 medically evaluated for injuries. U2 was cleared by hospital staff with only a small scrape and minor road rash to U2's left knee area. This CRASH is listed as a HIT and RUN so that I am able to submit it without being prevented by errors due to U2 not properly Identifying U1. I made my supervisor and Traffic Investigations aware of the details of this crash for guidance. DME Available

Unit #1 displayed damage to the front right corner and was removed from scene by private tow.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was stopped at the stop sign in the 100 block of Marion Pugh Drive at George Bush Drive West.  Unit 1 driver began to inch forward to turn on to George Bush Drive West and did not see Unit 2 attempting to cross Marion Pugh Drive on her electric scooter.  Unit 1 driver made contact with Unit 2 on her left side causing her to fall on to her right side.  Unit 1 driver stopped and asked Unit 2 if she was injured, however, Unit 2 informed her she was fine and told Unit 1 driver she did not need assistance.  Unit 2 was later treated by medical professionals for an injured foot.  The driver of unit 1 later called in and provided her information stating that she offered it to Unit 2 at the time, repeatedly, and was told each time that it was not needed.

crash_records.narrative: UNIT 1 was travelling eastbound through the alley in the 1500 block of Endicott DriveUNIT 2 was riding a scooter (non-motorized) throughout the alleyway and surrounding residences in the 1500 block of Endicott Drive.As UNIT 1 was approaching 1513 Endicott Drive, UNIT 2 rode his scooter from the top of the driveway of a residence to the north (Falmouth Drive) and entered the alleyway. As UNIT 2 entered the alleyway, UNIT 2 struck the LP of UNIT 1 near the back left (driver's side) passenger door. UNIT 2 then fell underneath the vehicle and UNIT 1 continued to drive not knowing that UNIT 2 was underneath the car. UNIT 1 stated he felt a bump as he was driving and then noticed a child in the alleyway. UNIT 1 rolled over the leg of UNIT 2 resulting in a possible broken femur.  UNIT 1 advised he did not see UNIT 2 exit into the alleyway and stopped immediately after seeing UNIT 2. I observed moderate damage on UNIT 1 where UNIT 2 struck the vehicle. Based on the damage, it appeared that UNIT 1 did not have enough time to react and stop before running over UNIT 2. Plano PD Crime Scene responded and photographed the damage to the vehicle.

crash_records.narrative: I met with Unit 1 at 27226 Ramblewood for a crash report she stated occurred at the intersection of Desert Links/Golf Bridge (Intersection is located inside a private gated subdivision) at approximately 1720 hours.  Unit 1 advised she was driving westbound on the 1300-Blk of Desert Links when Unit 2 (Electric scooter operated by Juvenile Sutton Grau) ran the stop sign from the 23800-Blk of Golf Bridge and crashed into her vehicle at  the intersection.  Unit 2 was heading southbound on the 23800-Blk of Golf Bridge and turning eastbound on the 1300-Blk of Desert Links.  Unit 1 advised she stopped her vehicle to render aid and was able to locate the juvenile's mother at her residence (3 houses north of listed intersection).  Unit 1 states she informed the juvenile's mother (Adrian Grau Ph# 210-788-9201) of the incident and then the mother stated to her, ""Let me guess he ran the stop sign again"".  Unit 1  provided the mother her information and the mother then transported her child to North Central Baptist for a broken leg he sustained including scrapes and bruises.

crash_records.narrative: UNIT 1 WAS EXITING THE DRIVEWAY NEAR 15424 FM 1825. UNIT 2 WAS ON AN ELECTRIC SCOOTER TRAVELING NB ALONG THE WESTERN SIDE (SB TRAVEL SIDE) OF FM 1825. THE WITNESS WAS DIRECTLY BEHIND UNIT 1. UNIT 1 INTENED TO EFFECT A RIGHT HAND TURN (TO SB OF 1825). UNIT 1 OBSERVED ITS LEFT AND SAW A CLEARING OF SB TRAFFIC. UNIT 1 DID NOT LOOK RIGHT. UNIT 1 ACCELERAED TO APPROXIMATELY 5 MPH BEFORE CONTACT WITH UNIT 2.UNIT 2 STATED THAT SHE WAS TRAVELING NORTH ON 1825 IN THE BIKE LANE ON THE SOUTBOUND SIDE. UNIT 2 STATED SHE WAS GOING APPROXIMATELY 15 MPH ON HER SCOOTER. UNIT 2 STATED SHE SAW THE CAR PULL INTO THE DRIVEWAY SO SHE SLAMED ON HER BRAKES. UNIT 2 STATED THAT THE CAR KEPT COMING ( INTO THE ROADWAY) AND HIT HER KNOCKING HER OFF HER SCOOTER.WITNESSES STATE UNIT 2 WAS TRAVELING ""AS FAST AS THE SCOOTER COULD GO"" AND DID NOT SLOW AS IT NEARED UNIT 1 AND THE DRIVEWAY PRIOR TO CONTACT WITH UNIT 1.UNIT 1 STRUCK UNIT 2. UNIT 1 SUFFERED MINOR DAMAGE TO ITS LICENSE PLATE AND NO INJURIES AT THE TIME OF INCIDENT. UNIT 2 WAS EJECTED FROM THE SCOOTER WITH APPARENT INJURES AND TRANSPORTED TO ROUND ROCK HOSPITAL BY EMS.

The driver of the Unit 2 stated that his left body was scratched as well as his left toe to which Q16 attended him. The driver of Unit 1 claimed no injuries. No Witness(es) were at the scene. The Unit 1 was driven from the scene.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1, towing unit 2, was traveling eastbound in the outside lane of W University Blvd. Unit 3, a horse ridden by a 18 yo male, was traveling westbound on the south shoulder of W University Blvd. Unit 3 became startled when a pack of dogs began to bark and began to back up into the outside lane of W University Blvd. Unit 1 attempted to avoid Unit 3 by changing lanes and maneuvering toward the inside lane, but Unit 3 continued to back up into oncoming traffic, eventually being struck by the back right tires of Unit 2. Units 1 and 2 came to rest in the upright position facing east in the outside lane of eastbound traffic. Unit 3 came to rest on its left side facing southwest in the outside lane of eastbound traffic.

crash_records.narrative: I was dispatched to the listed location for an accident minor, Upon arrival, I made contact with the parents of U2 at the hospital who explained to me what happened, the parents were not at the scene of the accident. The parents stated that U2 went to the store on his electric scooter. U2 was was traveling NB on the 800 block of E. Evans Rd. on the sidewalk crossing intersecting street Ridge Trace when U1 crashed into him. U2 stated that it happened so fast and was not sure but was hit primarily on his legs by U2. U1 stopped to check on U2 and left U2 with her first and last name with her phone number. U2 eventually took off back home on his scooter. When U2 got home his parents called police and transported him to the hospital.  Per medical staff, U2 does not have serious bodily injury. Currently U2 has no fractures, but does have swelling in both legs and abrasions.I contacted TID and spoke to Det. Garcia #2457 who advised of the steps to take regarding the incident and at this time to right up a crash report.Ofc. Contreras #1241 from north service area was able to make contact with U1 and get her information and statement.  U1 advised that she was exiting the neighborhood attempting to turn right onto evens rd. U1 stated that she stopped at the stop sign and then continued forward to check oncoming traffic. U1 advised that she did her yield and as she was turning onto the road she felt something hit her vehicle. U1 advised that a young male hit her vehicle. Vehicle had damage on the front passenger side door (dent). U1 stated that she asked U2 if he need ems or to be taken to a hospital. U1 stated that U2 declined and left the location. U1 stated that she gave U2 her information and got U2's phone number and first name. U2 stated he was fine and did not need anything. W1 stated that he was traveling south on evans rd when he was someone on a scooter traveling north bound at a high rate of speed on the sidewalk. W1 stated that he did not see the male and noticed that the male had hit O1's veh. W1 pulled over to assist.

On 1/17/2023 I was notified that an accident had occurred at Lake Belton Middle School 8818 Tarver Dr near the entrance to the west parking lot along S. Cedar Rd. I arrived and went to the nurses office where I met with the child that had been struck by a vehicle while on his Motorized Scooter. The student, identified as Alexander Embrey, stated that he was coming from his residence along the  sidewalk on Cedar Rd when he came to the crosswalk that is at the entrance to the school. He stopped momentarily as there were cars approaching the exit but he saw that they had stopped as if to allow him across. As he left the sidewalk and entered the crosswalk he was struck by Unit 1 and fell off of his Scooter. Alexander stated that a female driver exited the vehicle and asked if he was ok. He stated that he was fine and started to continue on to the boke rack to park his scooter. The female driver then left the parking lot and did not notify the local police department that a vehicle accident had occurred involving a child. A Witness saw the vehicle that committed the offense and wrote down a TX LP NGL1656 but this was done without staff identifying the male for follow-up. I ran the plate and it returned to KARLA PATRICIA BOLANOS. I found the individual in Belton ISD Skyward records system and called her. I asked if she had been involved in an accident at the intersection of Cedar and the parking lot yesterday and she stated that she had been. She advised that she had not seen the child and wasn't sure if she had actually hit the child. She stopped her vehicle and got out to check on the child and was told by the individual that he was fine. For this reason and because she wasn't actually sure if she hit him she left the scene.After talking to Mrs. Bolanos I advised her I would give her contact information to the mother of the child for resolutions to this incident.   Due to this information the active case 23000386 for fail to stop and give identifying information will be cancelled.

University Blvd, Brownsville, Texas 78520. On arrival to the B1 parking lot, I made contact with the two involved parties at the scene located inside the B1 parking lot near the W. University Blvd. (eastbound lane). As Unit#2 approached the sidewalk intersection with the B1 parking lot exit to W. University Blvd, Unit#2 collided with the Unit#1 by being struck with the front bumper of Unit#1 while inside the parking lot. Unit 2 was traveling south on a scooter in the 11800 block of Shadow Creek HS. Unit 2 entered the crosswalk on the east part of the private drive. The driver of Unit 1 turned left and proceeded to travel east into a private drive. When doing so, the driver of Unit 1 failed to yield to a pedestrian, who was in a crosswalk, and struck Unit 2. Damage to unit 2 was sustained to its front left. The operator of Unit 2 advised that he sustained minor injuries.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 2 was travelling eastbound on the westbound shoulder of US 281 on an electric scooter (motorized conveyance).   Unit 1 was traveling eastbound and passed a vehicle that was making a right turn by moving into the westbound lane and struck Unit 2 from behind in the shoulder of the highway.  The rider of Unit 2 was knocked over and sustained multiple minor injuries. Unit 2 came to a rest remaining on the shoulder of US 281.  Unit 1 fled from the scene without stopping.  Vehicle was unable to be located.  All means were exhausted.

crash_records.narrative: During my regular patrol routine, I was dispatched to the above listed location for an accident major involving a pedestrian. Upon making the listed location, I observed Unit 1 to be parked on N. St. Mary's St. and Unit 1 (Pedestrian) to be laying down right by Unit 1 at the front right side of the vehicle. When I arrived on scene, I observed SAFD medic unit #1 to be assisting the pedestrian for his injuries that he sustained from the accident. I made contact with the driver of Unit 1 and he stated the pedestrian who was operating the electric scooter, came of of nowhere and he tried to stop before he struck Unit 2. During my investigation, I made contact with W1 (W1 information is listed on my KC as W1) and W1 stated she was standing at the opposite side of the intersection when she observed Unit 2 run into Unit 1. W1 stated Unit 1 had the right of way from what she saw. Ofc. M. Montgomery #0319 spoke to the driver of Unit 1 and he stated Unit 2 along with a bunch of his friends where traveling in the opposite flow of traffic. Unit 1 driver stated he came to a complete stop and tried to get Unit 2's attention by flashing his high beams of his vehicle before Unit 2 struck him at the location. #0319 also spoke to friends that were with Unit 2 driver and they stated they were going in the opposite flow of traffic weaving in and out of traffic when Unit 2 struck Unit 1. During my investigation, I made contact with Det. B. Martinez #2477 who is assigned to Traffic Investigations. I advised her of the incident. CSI Salvatierra #9415 made the location and photographed the scene.During my investigation, I was informed by EMS Medic unit #1 that Unit 2 driver was being transported to SAMC for more medical treatment. I gave Unit 1 driver a case number for his records. SAFD was on scene and assisted in cleaning the incident location before police opened up the area for normal flow of traffic. No further actions taken.

crash_records.narrative: Driver 2 EB on 1400 W. Martin on an electric scooter-(Unit 2). Unit 1 NB on 400 block of San Marcos. Driver 2 reported that he believed driver 1 had waved him through as he turned off of W. Martin and made a right turn on to 400 San Marcos. Driver 1 stated he had not seen Driver 2 /Unit 2. Unit 1 and Unit 2 then collided at listed location.Driver 2 fell off of the electric scooter and sustained a small scrape to his left hand and scrape to left knee. Driver 2 did not want Driver 1 to call EMS/Ambulance. Driver 2 instead asked Driver 1 to give him a ride home. Driver 1 drove Driver 2 home and then relocated to his place of employment where he then called police to report accident.

crash_records.narrative: Unit #2, motorized conveyance (electric scooter) was traveling northbound on the sidewalk at the intersection of S. Gordon St./ E. Adoue St. Unit #1 was traveling southbound on S. Gordon St. and turned onto E. Adoue St. When Unit #1 turned east onto E. Adoue St. she collided with Unit #2. The rider of Unit #2 was struck by Unit #1 and flipped over the hood and landed, possibly face first onto the concrete. The rider of Unit #2 sustained multiple lacerations to his face and possibly broken bones in his arms and legs.The rider of Unit #2 was transported to HCA Clear Lake for medical treatment. The driver of Unit #1 was not issued a citation. Currently, it is unknown who failed to yield the right of way. Unit #1 was driven away from the scene.

crash_records.narrative: UNIT 2, WHO WAS OPERATING A LIME SCOOTER, COULD NOT TELL ME WHERE THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED. WE FOUND HIM IN THE 500 BLOCK OF CONGRESS AVE. HE POINTED SOUTH AND STATED THAT IT HAPPENED POSSIBLY 2 BLOCKS SOUTH AND POSSIBLY WEST FROM THAT LOCATION. HE STATED THAT HE DOESN'T REMEMBER WHAT HAPPENED, AND BELIEVED THAT HE LOST CONSCIOUSNESS AND THEN WOKE UP WHERE WE FOUND HIM. HE STATED THAT HE WAS ON A LIME SCOOTER DOING FOOD DELIVERY. HE LATER STATED THAT HE WAS IN THE BIKE LANE AND WAS ATTEMPTING TO MAKE A RIGHT HAND TURN, HE SAW A VEHICLE, THAT HE COULD NOT GIVE ANY DESCRIPTION OF, AND STATED ""I KNOW HE'S NOT GOING TO HIT ME"" AND THEN BELIEVES HE WAS STRUCK BY THE VEHICLE. I WAS UNABLE TO FIND THE SCOOTER AND HALO CAMERAS DID NOT CAPTURE THE INCIDENT OR WHERE UNIT 2 CAME FROM.

crash_records.narrative: At location U2 had already been taken by EMS to listed hospital due to severity of injury. I had Officer E. Sanchez #1034 translate U1's statement as far as what occurred (U1 speaks Spanish). U1 stated that she had been driving WB on 3100 Urban Crest Dr. when she then took a left turn to head SB onto  490 Pike Rd. U1 stated she did not see U2 on his motorized scooter until after the impact. U1 provided that U2 was traveling EB on Urban Crest Dr. crossing the intersection of Pike  Rd. U1 stated she was traveling the listed 30mph when the accident had taken place. U1 stated she was not using her phone at the time of accident, and was able to provide her phone record to ensure that statement. U1 had then moved her vehicle forward a few feet, left the car running and went to look for U2 to aid him. Bystanders whom did not witness the collision came out to assist U2 and call for EMS.U2 was not present to make a statement as he sustained major injuries. U2 was in critical condition, and I was advised by cover officer that U2 had sustained injuries to his head, abdomen and leg. Last update provided was that U2 was headed to surgery where he remained in critical condition.U2's motorized scooter was placed in the SAPD property room under barcode #990001900976.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was traveling E/B 1900 Bayside St.  Unit 2 (Phantom Electric Scooter) was traveling S/B 3800 Harston St.  Driver of Unit 2 failed to yield right of way- stop sign, entered the intersection and collided into the left front qtr of Unit 1.  Witness who was standing about 300 ft north of the accident scene, stated to I/O that he was looking S/B on Harston St. when he observed Driver of Unit 2 stop at the stop sign, look to his right and then to his left and then enter the intersection when Unit 2 was collided into by Unit 1.  The crash scene and all interviews were recorded on body worn camera.  The video and or photos were saved to evidence.com on ID #24-0561178.  Driver of Unit 2 and his passenger were seen by DFR but were not transported to an area hospital, but were transported by private conveyance.  I/O has witnesses information.

crash_records.narrative: Unit#1 was eastbound on Eldorado Drive stopped at the stop sign for Nevada Drive.  Unit#2 was traveling northbound on Nevada Drive attempting to cross the intersection at Eldorado Drive.  Unit#1 is a yellow Plano ISD school bus.  Unit#2 is an electric scooter.  Driver#1 stated he usually would travel southbound on Nevada Drivee and make a left turn onto eastbound Eldorado Drive.  Driver#1 stated he did not come that way this morning because of a trailer blocking the roadway and he made a decision to take a different route.  Driver#1 stated he was eastbound on Eldorado Drive and stopped at the stop sign at Nevada Drive.  Driver#1 stated he looked both ways before proceeding not see unit#2.  Driver#2 was not wearing a helmet.  Driver#2 stated as he was northbound on Nevada Drive, he saw unit#1 stop at the stop sign for eastbound Eldorado Drive but then start to proceed into the intersection.  Driver#2 stated he was wondering why unit#1 would enter the intersection and if he was going to stop.  Driver#1 fell off his scooter and slid underneath unit#1.  The right side rear wheels of unit#1  rolled over driver#2.  A witness who was behind unit#1 stated she saw unit#2 coming and could see the potential collision.  The witness confirmed that driver#2 fell off the scooter and slid underneath the school bus and did not run into the side of the school bus.  The witness was not sure which direction unit#2 came from but knew there was a car directly in front of her.  Driver#2 was transported to Medical City Plano for treatment.  I spoke with driver#2 at MCP.  He was awaiting more test.  Unit#1, ""Failed To Yield Right Of Way-After Stop""   On Friday, May 3, 2024 I added the names of the passengers of unit#1 to the crash report.  I also met with driver#1 and issued a citation for Fail To Yield Right Of Way - Intersection with Stop Sign/ Bodily Injury.

causal_chains.sentence: Driver#1 stated he did not come that way this morning because of a trailer blocking the roadway and he made a decision to take a different route.

crash_records.narrative: WHILE IN A MARKED PATROL CAR, A CITIZEN FLAGGED ME DOWN TO TELL ME THAT SOMEONE WAS HIT BY A CAR WHILE RIDING A SCOOTER IN THE AREA OF THE LISTED ACCIDENT ADDRESS. I RELOCATED TO THE AREA WHERE THE CITIZEN INFORMED ME THAT THE ACCIDENT TOOK PLACE. I CONTACTED UNIT 2 DRIVER ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE BLOCK. I OBSERVED UNIT 2, WITH DAMAGE TO THE FRONT WHEEL, LYING ON THE SIDEWALK A FEW FEET AWAY FROM UNIT 2 DRIVER.  UNIT 2 DRIVER APPEARED TO BE LIMPING. I ASKED UNIT 2 DRIVER TWICE IF HE NEEDED EMS. UNIT 2 DRIVER REFUSED EMS. UNIT 2 DRIVER STATED THAT WHILE TRAVELING SOUTHBOUND ON S. SANTA ROSA, UNIT 2 DRIVER WAS ATTEMPTING TO ACCESS THE PARKING LOT ON THE EAST SIDE OF THE BLOCK. UNIT 2 DRIVER STATED THAT THERE WAS A TRUCK IN THE MIDDLE OF THE INTERSECTION THAT WAS BLOCKING UNIT 2 DRIVER'S VIEW FOR ONCOMING NORTHBOUND TRAFFIC. THE TRUCK WAS TRAVELING EASTBOUND FROM URBAN LOOP ONTO NORTHBOUND S. SANTA ROSA. WHEN UNIT 2 DRIVER MERGED INTO THE NORTHBOUND LANES OF TRAVEL, UNIT 2 WAS STRUCK BY UNIT 1. UNIT 2 DRIVER STATED THAT UNIT 1 DRIVER STOPPED TO CHECK AND MAKE SURE UNIT 2 DRIVER WAS OKAY. WHEN UNIT 2 DRIVER ADVISED UNIT 1 DRIVER THAT HE WAS OKAY, UNIT 1 DRIVER  THEN LEFT THE LOCATION. UNIT 2 DRIVER WAS PROVIDED WITH THE CASE NUMBER. I NOTIFIED THE SCOOTER COMPANY AND PROVIDED THEM WITH THE CASE NUMBER.

crash_records.narrative: ***The vehicle listed as Unit 1 is due to Unit 2 being an electronic-type of conveyance.  However, Unit 1 is not necessarily considered the at-fault party due to conflicting stories.***  Unit 1 was traveling east at the 11900 block of Madera Run Pkwy at approximately 20 mph according to the driver and a witness that was also traveling eastbound on Madera Run, and several feet behind Unit 1.  As this Unit approached/crossed the intersection with Boundary Waters Ln, a child riding an electronic scooter-like conveyance, attempted to cross over Madera Run from Boundary Waters, not within the provided crosswalk on the west side of the intersection according to witness.  The child jumped off and away from the scooter and pushed/shoved it toward Unit 1.  Collision by the scooter was made with Unit 1 on the front left fender area with only minimal, if any, damage observed.  The scooter's front tire appeared to be unsalvageable.

crash_records.narrative: OFFICER BESS #9839 RESPONDED TO A HIGH PRIORITY CRASH AT THE INTERSECTION OF E 3RD STREET / N PLEASANT VALLEY ROAD. CALLER STATED HE WAS STRUCK BY A KNOWN SUBJECT WHILE HE WAS RIDING A SCOOTER. OFFICER BESS MADE CONTACT WITH UNIT 2 WHO STATED HE WAS TRAVELING ON THE EASTERN SIDEWALK OF N PLEASANT VALLEY ROAD TRAVELING SOUTHBOUND. UNIT 2 OBSERVED UNIT 1 SWERVE TOWARDS THE SIDEWALK. WHEN UNIT 2 ARRIVED AT THE INTERSECTION OF E 3RD STREET / N PLEASANT VALLEY ROAD, UNIT 2 MADE A DECISION TO HEAD EASTBOUND ON E 3RD STREET TO GET AWAY FROM UNIT 1. UNIT 2 WAS THEN STRUCK BY UNIT 1 AT THE INTERSECTION WHICH CAUSED UNIT 2 TO FALL TO THE GROUND. UNIT 1 THEN WENT DOWN THE STREET FROM THE SCENE HEADING EASTBOUND ON E 3RD STREET.UNIT 2 RECEIVED A PHONE CALL FROM UNIT 1 DRIVER AND UNIT 2 DRIVER SAID SOMETHING TO THE EFFECT OF ""YOU JUMPED IN FRONT OF ME I DID NOT HIT YOU!"" UNIT 1 DRIVER MET UP WITH OFFICERS AT A NEARBY LOCATION TO PROVIDE HIS INSURANCE AND DRIVERS LICENSE INFO.UNIT 1 STATED THAT HE WAS TRAVELING SOUTHBOUND ON N PLEASANT VALLEY ROAD WHEN UNIT 1 DECIDED TO GET IN FRONT OF HIS VEHICLE, CAUSING THE COLLISION. UNIT 2 THEN BECAME IRATE AT UNIT 1 WHICH CAUSED UNIT 1 TO FEAR FOR HIS SAFETY AND GO DOWN THE STREET. UNIT 1'S VEHICLE HAD NO DAMAGE TO IT FROM THE COLLISION. OFFICERS PROVIDED UNIT 1 WITH ALL OF UNIT 2'S PERTINENT INFORMATION.

Unit 2 (an electric scooter) was being operated in the space between the allowed parking space and Westbound lane. Unit 1 was traveling Westbound on 2600 Wyoming, proceeded to make a right turn, colliding with Unit 2 and continuing onto 700 Rosewood. Driver of Unit 1 then fled the scene in Unit 1. The report was taken at Central Regional Command. There were no witnesses located. The operator of Unit 2 indicated injuries to his elbows, knees and right hip area. Cross reference incident 24-194155.

crash_records.narrative: ON 07/17/24, I ARRIVED ON THE SCENE OF A CRASH INVOLVING A VEHICLE AND ELECTRIC SCOOTER RIDER. THE CRASH OCCURRED APPROXIMATELY 15 FEET NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF E CESAR CHAVEZ ST. AND N IH35 SVRD NB.DURING THE INVESTIGATION I LEARNED THAT VEH #1 TURNED NB FROM CESAR CHAVEZ. VEH #1 TRAVELED NB IN THE INSIDE LANES AND STRUCK THE SCOOTER AND IT'S OPERATOR (VEH #2).  THE SCOOTER RIDER WAS AWAKE AND ONLY HAD MINOR INJURIES TO THE RIGHT HIP AND BOTH LEGS. SHE REFUSED EMS MULTIPLE TIMES ON SCENE AND STATED THAT SHE WOULD SEEK TREATMENT ON HER OWN.BOTH SUBJECTS GAVE DIFFERING ACCOUNTS OF WHAT OCCURRED. THE DRIVER VEH #1 SAID THAT HE TURNED WHILE HE HAD THE GREEN LIGHT. HE STATED THAT HE DID NOT NOTICE THE SCOOTER RIDER AND WAS NOT SURE IF THERE WAS A GREEN LIGHT FOR THE PEDESTRIAN CROSSWALK.THE SCOOTER RIDER SAID THAT SHE HAD THE GREEN LIGHT TO CROSS IN THE CROSSWALK AND THAT THE NUMBER WERE POSSIBLY COUNTING DOWN WHEN SHE CROSSED. THERE WAS NO VIDEO EVIDENCE READILY AVAILABLE TO PROVE OR DISPROVE ANY OF THESE STATEMENTS. BROWN FORMS WERE EXCHANGED AND NO CITATIONS WERE ISSUED DUE TO THE DIFFERING ACCOUNTS GIVEN BY EACH PARTY.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 is a Red 2013 Ram 1500 TX License Plate PTG9981. Unit 2 is a black motorized scooter, (Brand) IScooter, (Model) Electric Scooter i9 with a serial plate but no number found.Unit 1 was traveling northbound in the 300 block of N St Felix Ave and stopped at the stop sign at the intersection of W Sloan St and N St Felix Ave with the intent to turn left (West) onto the 1900 Block of W Sloan St. Unit 2 was traveling southbound in the 400 block of N St Felix Ave and stopped at the stop sign at the intersection of W Sloan St and N St Felix Ave with the intent of continuing straight to the 300 Block of N St Felix Ave.  The 300 Block of N St Felix Ave is a public roadway. The 400 Block of N St Felix Ave is owned by Tarleton State University. The 1800 and 1900 Blocks of W Sloan St, including the intersection with N St Felix Ave, are owned by Tarleton State University. Operators of both Unit 1 and Unit 2 stated that a non-involved vehicle crossed the intersection of W Sloan St and N St Felix Ave traveling westbound, causing both Unit 1 and Unit 2 to stop at the stop sign. Operator of Unit 2 stated that the operator of Unit 1 hesitated, so Operator of unit 2 felt she was given the right of way by Unit 1. The Operator of Unit 2 stated that she saw the blinker of Unit 1 indicating a left turn. The Operator of Unit 2 then proceeded to cross W Sloan St.  Operator of Unit 1 did not see Unit 2 and initiated a left turn (West) onto W Sloan St. striking Unit 2, causing the operator and passenger of Unit 2 to completely go under Unit 1. Operator of Unit 2 stated that she felt both the front and rear tires of Unit 1 run over her. The operator of Unit 1 stated that she did not see Unit 2 stopped at the stop sign. The operator of Unit 1 claims when making her left turn (westbound) onto W Sloan St, she could not see due to the sun in her eyes. Operator of Unit 1 stated that she only saw a dark figure out of the corner of her eye before she felt bumps as she drove over the operator and passenger of Unit 2. Officers asked the operator of unit 1 if she was on her electronic device at the time of, or just prior to, the crash. The operator stated that she was not using an electronic device. The only witness to the crash stated she only saw the impact and not the events leading up to the crash. The witness stated she saw Unit 1 strike Unit 2 and saw Unit 1 drive over the operator and passenger of Unit 2. The witness and the operator of Unit 2 were asked if they saw the operator of Unit 1 using an electronic device at the time of the crash. Both the witness and the operator of Unit 2 stated they did not see Unit 1 using an electronic device. Officers looked for possible cameras in the area. But could not locate any in that area.

Unit 1 was northbound S. Pearl Expy. Unit 2, a motorized scooter, was eastbound Main St. According to a witness whose name and phone number appears in the comments section of the Incident Detail Report 24-1749005, Unit 2 disregarded the stop and go signal (ran the red light) and collided front to the left passenger of unit 1 in the intersection of Main St. and S. Pearl Expy. Interviews with unit 1 driver, the scooter operator and witness (phone) were recorded on investigating officer's body worn camera and saved at evidence.com on the previously referenced incident number. On 09/26/2024 a second witness, who could not be reached earlier, stated that unit 1 had a green light and unit 2 ran the red light. The Dallas Police Safety Camera at the southeast corner of the intersection was pointed at the parking lot on the southeast corner of the intersection and did not capture the crash.

Axon fleet and BWC used.

Unit 1 was traveling north in the parking lot lane and approaching Brackenridge Road. Upon reaching the end of the parking lot lane and the crosswalk, that is part of the bike and walking pedestrian lane, the vehicle collided with Unit 2 (pedestrian on a bird scooter). After the collision, Unit 2 and pedestrian fell on the ground and Unit 1 drove away (East bound) from the motor vehicle collision without stopping to render aid to the injured person. The driver of Unit 2 suffered small cuts and or lacerations to his nose at the time of the crash. The driver of Unit 2 was not transported by EMS to the hospital for his injuries but was transported to the UTSA wellness clinic for first aid. The driver of Unit 2 said he would notify the UTSAPD if he was medically treated for any late onset injuries that caused from the motor vehicle accident.

crash_records.narrative: While on routine patrol, I was flagged down by the driver of Unit 2 and the passenger of Unit 2. I asked what happened, and they claimed that Unit 1 hit them as they were passing the outlet of 515 S Frio, on the sidewalk, as Unit 1 was turning out of the private property at 515 S Frio St. I pulled into the parking lot and spoke with both Units. Unit 2's occupants refused EMS. Unit 2's driver claimed that he was hit on the ankle by unit 1. Unit 2's driver claimed that he had pain to the ankle (There was minor redness), but he refused EMS. Unit 2's passenger denied injury. I spoke to Unit 1's driver. Unit 1 stated he was pulling out of 515 S Frio to turn on to the main road. Unit 1 stated he checked both ways and began to turn out of the private drive. As Unit 1 was pulling out, he had collided with Unit 2. Unit 1 had very minimal damages. After listening to both units side of the story, it appeared Unit 1 caused the collision due to driver inattention. Both parties were issued CRASH sheets. The driver of Unit 2 was found to have warrants. Unit 2's driver was arrested.***Unit 2 is an electric scooter***DMEA

crash_records.narrative: This is the narrative of the involved-officer:""On Monday, 12/30/2024, at approximately 1200 hours, I, Officer T. Kiser #3530 was driving Police Unit 1808, eastbound, in the 2400 block of Timber Ridge. I was traveling, heading to the Frisco Athletic Center for my shift. As I approached Timber Ridge and Trailside Drive, I quickly observed a white juvenile male, riding an electric scooter, fail to stop at the stop sign at Timber Ridge and Trailside Drive. I observed the male operating the electric scooter at a high rate of speed and enter on Timber Ridge. As I approached the male, I pressed my brakes and swerved to the left, attempting to miss the male on the scooter. I then observed the male, on the scooter, strike the right side my patrol car. I then continued on Timber Ridge where I eventually was able to come to a stop. I then observed the male get up from the ground and start moving towards the sidewalk. I then notified dispatch, activated my emergency lights, and reversed to the intersection to block off the damaged scooter.""

actions.sentence: As I approached Timber Ridge and Trailside Drive, I quickly observed a white juvenile male, riding an electric scooter, fail to stop at the stop sign at Timber Ridge and Trailside Drive.
I then continued on Timber Ridge where I eventually was able to come to a stop.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was traveling westbound in the middle lane of E Mockingbird Ln approaching the intersection of McMillian Ave. Unit 2, a motorized conveyance (person standing on an electric scooter), was traveling from the north side of E Mockingbird to the south side. Unit 2 used a pedestrian ramp and entered the intersection and began to cross the roadway. Unit 1 collided front concentrated with Unit 2, knocking Unit 2 to the ground. Unit 2 was transported to Texas Health Presbyterian Dallas and was pronounced deceased. A Witness stated that Unit 1 run the red light prior to colliding with Unit 2. Due to the stop line being further back than a normal intersection, I/O is unable to determine if he legally entered the intersection or not prior to the light turning red. I/O is also unable to determine if Unit 2 had the right of way.  Vehicle Crimes Unit came to the scene to collect evidence. See related police report 015822-2025. This is Traffic Fatality #6 for the City of Dallas in 2025. Dallas County Medical Examiner is using case number 25-02010. This report is incomplete pending further investigation and the toxicology results for Unit 2.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was stopped at a stop sign at the intersection of Towne Lake Pkwy & Cypress North Houston Rd. facing NB.  Unit 2, a pedestrian on a motorized scooter, was traveling WB across the crosswalk.  Driver of unit 1 stated he looked both ways and believed it was clear before attempting to turn left onto Cypress North Houston Rd.  The operator of Unit 2, stated he was ridding across the crosswalk, WB, towards school. As he traveled infront of Unit 1, he was struck.  Driver of Unit 1 stayed on scene, provided his information, and assisded the operator of Unit 2 by driving him to school where he would be safeand get checked out. It doesnt appear there was any negligence on either person.  Unit 1sustained damage to its' FC, and the operator of Unit 2 sustained minor injuries to the left side of his body.

crash_records.narrative: Person 2, riding an electric scooter, recklessly crossed an unmarked intersection outside of a designated crosswalk. At the same time, Person 1 was positioned in the right-turn lane, attempting to make a legal turn. As Person 2 crossed in front of her vehicle, Person 1 was forced to brake abruptly to avoid a collision. Despite the effort to stop, Person 2 was struck on the right side of their scooter. Based on the investigation, Person 1 was placed at fault under Transportation Code, Title 7, Vehicles and Traffic, Subtitle C, Rules of the Road, Chapter 552, Pedestrians. Specifically, Sec. 552.005 regarding Crossing at Point Other Than Crosswalk.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 2 stated that he was on the sidewalk on his electric scooter facing eastbound on the 9100 block of Timber Path.  Unit 2 stated that he was stopped at the red light waiting for the traffic light to turn green.  Unit 2 stated that Unit 1 was on the roadway at the 9100 block of Timber Path also facing eastbound.   Unit 2 stated that when the traffic light turned green he proceeded into the intersection crossing the 6100 block of Tezel Rd.  Unit 2 stated that Unit 1 also proceeded into the intersection and started to turn left into the 6100 block of Tezel Rd.  Unit 2 stated that Unit 1 failed to yield the right of way to Unit 2 while turning left.  Unit 2 stated that he jumped off the electric scooter before Unit 1 collided with Unit 2.  Unit 2 stated that EMS cleared him at the scene, but when he was home his left knee started to hurt.  Unit 2 stated that he was mother was going to take him to Methodist Hospital to get checked.  Unit 2 stated that the driver of Unit 1 left the scene, but did provide Unit 2 with Unit 1's driver's license and insurance information.  DMEA

crash_records.narrative: The 400 Block of South Copia Street is a two lane roadway, utilized for South and Northbound traffic only with a pedestrian crosswalk and a stop signal. Unit 1 was traveling Southbound on lane number one and Unit 2 (electric scooter) was traveling Westbound on the pedestrian crosswalk. Driver of Unit 1 advised that she stop at the stop signal and then proceeded to travel Southbound when Unit 2 was traveling Westbound on the crosswalk and was unable to stop causing a collision against Unit 1. Driver of Unit 2 advised that he was traveling Westbound on the crosswalk when Unit 1 was standing still blocking the crosswalk causing him to collide against Unit 1.  Driver of Unit 1 reported no injuries at the scene. Driver of Unit 2 complained of pain on the full body emphasizing on the head. Driver of Unit 2 refused Fire Medical services, no witnesses were located at the scene and all Units were removed by the owners.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 2 (electric scooter) was in the bike lane going east on Ross street before turning north in the crosswalk on Bizzell street. Unit 2 did not stop at any of the stop signs that are located at the exits to both roadways. Unit 1 was travelling south on Bizzell street attempting to turn east to continue down Bizzell street. Unit 2 did not stop or observe Unit 1 turning into the roadway and had the rear end of his scooter struck by Unit 1. Unit 2 falls over, then gets back onto the scooter and continues to go North on Bizzell street. Unit 1 stopped and was unable to locate Unit 2. Unit 2 called EMS services and was placed into Ambulance care approximately 10 minutes later. Unit 2 was transported to a emergency room for a minor injury and then issued a citation for ""Disregard Stop Sign.""

On April 11th, 2025 at approximately 1333 hours, I responded to an Accident Major at 476 Ross Street. I arrived on scene and spoke with Unit 1. Unit 1 advised he was driving his electric scooter eastbound on Ross Street at approximately 17mph when he saw Unit 2 apply their brakes in front of them. Unit 2 was also heading eastbound on Ross Street, but had come to a stop as they were yielding to those with the right-of-way. Unit 1 explained he was too close and did not have enough time or distance after he applied his brakes to stop before his scooter hit the back of Unit 2. Unit 2 sustained small dents and scratches on the back doors. Unit 1 sustained small dents and scratches to his scooter. The person in Unit 1 sustained minor injuries such as cuts, but refused to go to the hospital for further evaluation. Unit 2 sustained no injuries. No citations were issued.

crash_records.narrative: UNIT #1 WAS DRIVING WESTBOUND ON W 24TH STREET. UNIT #2 WAS RIDING SOUTHBOUND ON NUECES ST. THE ACCIDENT OCCURED AT THE 3 WAY (T) INTERSECTION OF W 24TH STREET AND NUECES STREET. THE ACCIDENT OCCURRED AT NIGHT BUT THE STREETS WERE LIGHTED. THE SPEED OF TRAVEL OF BOTH UNIT #1 AND UNIT #2 ARE UNKNOWN. UNIT #2 IS A LIME ELECTRIC SCOOTER.AS UNIT #1 WAS DRIVING WEST ON W 24TH ST. AND PASSING NUECES ST., UNIT #2 WAS TRAVELING SOUTH ON NUECES ST. AND MADE A WIDE RIGHT TURN ONTO W 24TH ST. UNIT #2 PASSED ON THE LEFT OF A STOPPED CAR WAITING TO TURN RIGHT ONTO W 24TH STREET. UNIT #2 COLLIDED WITH THE BACK RIGHT QUARTER PANEL OF UNIT #1.THE OPERATOR OF UNIT #1 FELL OF OF THE LIME SCOOTER AND SUSTAINED SOME HEAD INJURIES. AFD AND EMS PROVIDED MEDICAL SERVICES, BUT UNIT #2 REFUSED TO BE TRANSPORTED TO A HOSPITAL.BROWN FORMS WERE PROVIDED TO UNIT #1 AND UNIT #2.VIDEO FROM UTPD CAMERA POINTED TOWARDS W 24TH ST / NUECES ST SHOWS SOUTHBOUND NUECES TRAFFIC STOPPED WHEN UNIT #2 SLOWS, BUT DOES NOT STOP AND MAKES A WIDE RIGHT TURN AROUND A STOPPED BLACK SEDAN THAT WAS WAITING TO TURN WESTBOUND ON W 24TH ST. UNIT #1 CAN BE SEEN BRAKING/SWERVING IN AN ATTEMPT TO AVOID THE SCOOTER.THE LIGHT CYCLE FOR THE WESTBOUND W 24TH ST APPEARS TO BE TIMED TO ONE MINUTE. THE COLLISION OCCURS JUST BEFORE THE ONE MINUTE MARK. TRAFFIC LIGHTS CANNOT BE SEEN FROM THE VIDEO RENDERING IT INCONCLUSIVE WHAT SIGNAL EACH DIRECTION OF TRAVEL WAS FACED WITH WHEN THE COLLISION OCCURRED. BOTH UNIT #1 AND #2 CLAIM THAT EACH HAD A GREEN LIGHT FOR THEIR RESPECTIVE DIRECTION OF TRAVEL.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1, a maroon 2008 Dodge Ram 1500 TX LP-RYL0110, towing Unit 2, a tan 1997 Toyota Camry TX LP-SLZ8116, was traveling northbound in the main lane of travel on SH-5/N Powell Parkway. Unit 3, an electric scooter, was traveling northbound on the northbound shoulder of SH-5/N Powell Parkway. Unit 3 turned when unsafe as it attempted to turn left from the shoulder of N Powell Parkway onto Rhett Rd and struck the side of Unit 1. The impact caused the rider of Unit 3 to fall off at which time it was struck by the front end of Unit 2. The rider of Unit 3 was pronounced dead on scene at approximately 12:56 PM. There were no other reported injuries.

crash_records.narrative: On 06/04/2025 at about 1739 hours, Unit #1 was traveling southbound in the 700 block of S. MacArthur Blvd in lane #2 of 4.  The southbound lanes of S. MacArthur Blvd has two left turn lanes to turn eastbound onto E. Belt Line Road.  Lane #1 which is closest to the center median and Lane #2.  There is a dashed white line painted on the ground to direct lane #1 turning left to stay in the left most lane of E. Belt Line Road.  Unit #2 which is a GoTrax motor assisted scooter, was in lane #1. Unit #1 and Unit #2 began to make a left turn onto E. Belt Line Road, Unit #2 changed lanes when unsafe leaving the designated lane of travel and entered into the path of travel for Unit #1 that was turning into the right most lane of E. Belt Line Road.Texas Transportation Code #551.352: Unit #2 which is a motor-assisted scooter may be operated only on a street for which the posted speed limit is 35 miles per hour or less.  The speed limit on E. Belt Line Road which Unit #2 was attempting to turn onto is 50 miles per hour. City of Coppell Municipal 8-5-5: a person may not operate a motor assisted scooter on any alley, street, or highway within the city.Witness: Jamilah Gist-Person 469-765-7830

crash_records.narrative: On 06-19-25, at approximately 1600 hours, Unit 2 was traveling along the east curb line on the side walk of 700 Alma Drive and approached the intersection of W. Plano Parkway. Unit 2 entered traffic in the crosswalk on a green light when Unit 1 which was driving west bound on W. Plano Parkway violated the red signal light and struck Unit 2, knocking the rider of the unit (electric scooter). The rider of Unit 2 recovered and rode to his nearby home. Simultaneously, Unit 1 possibly driven by a white female (blonde) and accompanied by a possible Hispanic male fled the scene in Unit 1, west bound on W. Plano Parkway without exchanging information. The rider of Unit 2 was transported to Medical City Plano hospital for pain to his right wrist, both sides of his torso, abrasions on his right leg and head. There appeared to be no life threatening injuries. See offense report for hit and run details.

crash_records.narrative: Unit one, a black electric scooter, was traveling on the sidewalk in the westbound direction within the 500 block of Friendship Lane. Unit one, while still on the sidewalk, came to the intersection of Friendship Lane and South Washington Street. Unit one's brakes did not properly engage. This caused the scooter to continue beyond the sidewalk and into the intersection. Unit two was stopped at the intersection of Friendship Lane and South Washington Street. They were in the outside lane facing the westbound direction. When the traffic signal turned green, unit two continued to turn northbound onto South Washington Street. At this time, unit one continued off of the sidewalk and failed to yield ROW when not in the crosswalk. Unit one struck unit two's front right quarter panel. The driver of Unit one fell off of the scooter and into the roadway.

crash_records.narrative: On 07/02/2025 at about 1420 hours, Lakeway police officers responded to a collision involving a car and a child on an electric scooter at Highlands Blvd and Trophy Drive. I contacted a witness on scene who was identified as Todd Clark. He told me he was stopped about one car length behind a silver Lexus SUV who was stopped at the stop sign in the right westbound lane on Highlands Blvd at Trophy Drive. Clark said the SUV started to pull forward when the girl on a motorized scooter came flying and ran into the side of the SUV, I asked him if the girl came from the driveway (Trophy Drive) from World of Tennis, he said, ""Yes."" Clark said he saw the girl buckle and go under the car, he didn't know if she ended up under the tire. Clark and his wife Grace, stopped, and assisted at the scene.The SUV involved in the collision is a silver 2016 Lexus LX570 with Texas license plate GWW2876. I looked at the Lexus and noticed a large dent in the right front fender.The other driver who was stopped in the left lane next to the SUV was identified as Nancy Shilling. Shilling commented that she saw the SUV hit the girl, I told her that the girl actually ran into the side of the SUV. Shilling didn't see where the girl came from. The driver of the SUV was identified by a Texas driver's license as Ningguang Xu. I asked Ningguang if he saw where the girl came from, he said he has no idea. He said he stopped and then pressed the gas pedal and was then hit (by the scooter). Ningguang told me he has a dashcam, he was unable to show me the video and said he needed to get the memory card and upload the video. I gave him my business card and asked him to email me the video. The girl on the scooter was identified as 12-year-old Charlotte Seif. Her dad arrived on scene, he was identified as Alexander Seif. Charlotte told me she was coming back from Lakeway Market and was riding on the westbound sidewalk and was going to cross the crosswalk at Highlands Blvd and Trophy Dr. She said she was trying to stop and then, from what she remembers is just crossing right into him. I asked her if she stopped at the crosswalk, she said she stopped for a second and the car wasn't going. I asked her if she was coming down the driveway from World of Tennis, she said she did not come down the driveway and that she was on the sidewalk. Looking at the amount of damage to the SUV, I don't believe she stopped at the crosswalk. Alexander stated those scooters have some ""serious pick-up"", but he agreed that looking at the damage to the SUV, there was some momentum from the scooter.    I spoke to Todd Clark again and asked him if the girl came from the ""World of Tennis"" driveway, he replied he wasn't 100% positive and looked at the scene again. Todd walked over to the crosswalk and said it was in this area that the collision occurred, at the crosswalk. He said he was stopped back there where the tree and rock are and ""it was like a bullet came out"" talking about the scooter. I asked Todd if she passed him coming down the sidewalk, he said she did not come this way (down the sidewalk). Todd said he just saw a blur coming this way (from the driveway) and smacked the car.Charlotte told me she did not come from the driveway, she was on the sidewalk when she came up to the crosswalk.Looking at the amount of damage to the SUV, I don't believe Charlotte was stopped on the sidewalk at the crosswalk before she started to cross. Based on my investigation, I would find Charlotte at fault for this collision, I did not issue her any citations.Charlotte had an abrasion on her left hip, she was not complaining of any other injuries. Her father will take her to a doctor if needed.At the time of this report, I have not yet received the video from Ningguang. End of report.Rod Morse #752

UNIT 2 WAS TRAVELING EASTBOUND ON UNIVERSITY DR. UNIT 1 WAS TRAVELING SOUTHBOUND ON TEXAS AVE. UNIT 2 BEGAN TO CROSS THE CROSSWALK ON HIS ELECTRIC SCOOTER AS UNIT 1 DROVE THROUGH THE INTERSECTION. UNIT 2 STATED HE DID NOT CONFIRM THE CROSSWALK WAS DISPLAYING THE WHITE GO ICON BEFORE HE STARTED MOVING, RATHER HE BEGAN TO MOVE WHEN HE HEARD THE AUTOMATED CLICKING NOISE OF THE SIGNAL LIGHT. A WITNESS, IDENTIFIED BY NAME AND DOB AS HUNTER SLATON, WHO WAS AT THE REDLIGHT FACING EAST BOUND ON UNIVERSITY, STATED THAT THE CROSSWALK SIGNAL THAT UNIT 2 WAS TRYING TO CROSS WAS SHOWING THE RED STOP ICON. UNIT 1 STATED SHE HAD A GREEN LIGHT AND DID NOT SEE UNIT 2 UNTIL HE WAS INFRONT OF HER. UNIT 1 TRIED TO STOP, BUT STILL COLLIDED WITH UNIT 2, WHO WHEN HIT, FELL ONTO THE HOOD OF UNIT 1 AND THEN ROLLED OFF ONTO THE CONCRETE MEDIUM OF TEXAS AVE. crash_records.narrative: This accident reportedly occurred at 19600 Plantation Myrtle Drive.Unit #1 was traveling NB at the 5500 block of Plantation Forest Drive.Unit #1 advised she turned left WB at the 19600 block of Plantation Myrtle Drive. Unit #2 advised he was traveling next to the curb of the roadway in a bicycle lane when Unit #1 turned and struck him riding on his scooter.During the investigation I observed this road to be a two-lane roadway with an unprotected median that travels east and west with no designated lane for bicycle travel. Unit #2 showed a video of where the incident occurred which was not consistent of where he advised he was riding his scooter when struck. The video shows this accident occurring in the middle of the roadway on Plantation Myrtle Drive, not against the curb.Cy-Fair Medic 505 arrived on scene and cleared all parties of injuries at this time.No witnesses came forward.No citation was issued due to inconsistence in both parties' statements.My BWC was activated.

crash_records.narrative: On 9/13/2025, I was dispatched to the intersection of Stillwater Pkwy and Silver Pointe for a report of a crash involving juveniles on a scooter. Upon arrival, I made contact with Juvenile Michael Tijerina and Juvenile Stephen Oquendo. Juvenile Michael and Juvenile Stephen stated they were traveling northbound on Stillwater Pkwy. The juveniles stated they were riding their electric scooter on the crosswalk to cross the street when they were hit from behind by a black sedan. The impact caused the juveniles to fall off of the scooter. The juveniles stated the driver of the sedan reversed their vehicle, apologized and quickly sped away without stopping to render aid or provide any type of information. The juveniles suffered minor scratches from the accident but refused to be seen by EMS at this time.The UNIT 2 vehicle was a ""Ninebot"" Segway scooter. Due to the vehicle not being a motorized vehicle driving on a public roadway, a license plate and insurance was not required.

crash_records.narrative: On 09/25/2025, at approximately 1835hrs, U1 was stopped at a stop sign facing south in the 1900 block of University Ave. The driver reported they looked both ways before turning right onto Martin Luther King Blvd, when they proceeded through the intersection colliding with U2 (electric scooter). U2 made contact with the front center of U1 causing minor scrapes at the 12 o'clock position. U2 was ejected from their scooter and fell onto the ground in the middle of the roadway. U2 was traveling east bound at an unknown rate of speed on the northside curbline in the 200 block of W Martin Luther King Blvd before crossing the intersection of University Ave. U2 had scratches on his right shin, right knee, right shoulder, right side of face, and on his right thumb. U2 did not appear to have any serious injuries.

crash_records.narrative: See related SN# 136305-2025.  Unit 1 was traveling in a S/W direction in the second lane, S/E of the N/W curb 1800 Ross Ave.  Unit 2 (Electric Scooter) was traveling in a N/W direction thru a marked crosswalk while crossing the S/W traffic lanes of Ross Ave.  Driver of Unit 1 disregarded a stop and go signal (ran a red signal light) crossed the street intersection, entered the crosswalk and struck the front right of Unit 1 to the right side of Unit 2. Driver of Unit 2 was injured but refused to be transported to an area hospital by DFR, but will be taken to an area hospital by his wife.  At the scene, I/O found witnesses.  Witness # 1 stated that she was a passenger in a vehicle and was traveling behind Unit 1.  When I/O asked, what color was the signal light, Witness stated that the signal light was red.  Witness #2 was standing on the S/W sidewalk and was stopped for red pedestrian crossing signal.  I/O has both witnesses information.  I/O also located video from the Hunt Building (1900 N. Akard St) that has surveillance several cameras near the collision location.  The video I/O saw shows that Unit 2 had entered the marked crosswalk on a flashing red hand pedestrian signal, as the countdown timer was counting down.  The video also shows that the signal light for 700 N. St. Paul St. was a solid green.  The crash scene and all interviews were recorded on body worn camera.  The video and or photos were saved to evidence.com on ID #25-1687384.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was northbound and stopped at the stop sign at the intersection of South Pendleton Dr. and the eastbound lanes of Commonwealth Blvd. Unit 2 was traveling westbound on the sidewalk of Commonwealth Blvd. entering the crosswalk from the east crossing South Pendleton Dr. in a westbound direction in front of Unit 1 from right to left. Unit 1 attempted to turn right and failed to yield right of way to Unit 2 striking Unit 2 and causing the operator, passenger, and electric scooter to fall. Unit 1 driver then panicked and depressed the accelerator instead of the brake pedal. Unit 1 ran over the 9 year old passenger of Unit 2 with the left wheels of Unit 1 causing major injuries to her lower extremities and pelvis. The operator of Unit 2 was pushed to the ground causing minor injuries to his extremities. The electric scooter was also run over causing incapacitating damage.

crash_records.narrative: On Thursday, September 18th, 2025, at approximately 1050 hrs, AO Mraz was dispatched to a Major Collision at 299 W Dean Keeton St, a public street, at the intersection with Whitis Avenue. Upon arrival, AO Mraz discovered that Offender Langford had disregarded a traffic control device at the listed location and ran the red light while riding a scooter.  IO Macias was in his company work vehicle in the far-right WB lane when he collided into Offender Langford, knocking her off her scooter.  Offender Langford sustained multiple scratches, scrapes, and bruises, along with a possible concussion from the collision.  AFR Engine 3 responded, along with EMS Demand 3, who transported Offender Langford to Dell Seton Medical Center.IO Macias was uninjured but shaken up from the incident. IO Macias was operating a company vehicle at the time of the collision.  The vehicle was not damaged.  Video was captured by a camera located at Dean Keeton & Whitis and also from Unit 1's in-car camera system. Offender Langford was issued Citation #CA-013861 for Disregard of Traffic Control Device  Ran Red Light.

crash_records.narrative: At the listed date and time, I was dispatched to the listed location for a accident major. I made location and observed EMS with one party of the accident.I spoke to the driver of unit 1 (U1). U1 stated he was traveling northbound on Augusta St.  U1 stated he observed unit 2 in front of him. U1 stated he was running late to work and attempted to go around unit 2. U1 stated he was unsure why but unit 2 suddenly went left. U1 stated unit 2 made contact with unit 1's mirror. U1 provided me with a valid drivers license and insurance. I spoke to the driver of unit 2, a VEO electric scooter, (U2). U2 stated he was also traveling northbound on Augusta street. U2 stated he attempted to make a left turn onto a private road. U2 stated he heard a car engine accelerate and was stuck from behind. U2 stated the collision cause him to fall off the scooter. U2 was seen by EMS and cleared. U2 stated he was in pain from his elbows, left leg and back due to the accident. I observed scrapes on U2's elbows and left shin. U2 was advised to see a medical professional if his condition worsens.Both U1 and U2 were provided case cards.DMEA

crash_records.narrative: San Antonio Street is a 2 lane, undivided roadway running North and South. Martin Luther King Blvd. is a 4-lane divided roadway with an unprotected median/turn lane. Unit 1 is a White Toyota Camry which was stopped at the intersection of San Antonio Street and Martin Luther King Blvd. Unit 2 is a Lime Scooter which was being operated by Sobol. Unit 2 was utilizing the bike lane, heading West bound on the East bound lanes of Martin Luther King Blvd. Unit 1 made a right turn from San Antonio Street, onto Martin Luther King Blvd. to head East. Sobol complained of pain to his left thumb and back but denied medical attention.
