Unit 2 was traveling southbound the 14800 block of Ralston Road when Unit 1 entered the moving lanes of traffic. Unit 2 attempted to utilize her brakes however, Unit 1 still struck the front right panel of Unit 2. A witness, Tyler Pushia (832-798-4743), advised he was stopped just north of the apartment complex driveway. Witness stated he observed Unit 1 enter the moving lanes of traffic however failed to clear both lanes. Witness advised Unit 1 noticed Unit 2 approaching when he had already entered the moving lanes of traffic. Witness advised Unit 1 attempted to stop however still struck the front right panel of Unit 2. Unit 1 was wearing football pads and a helmet during the accident.

crash_records.narrative: Unit # 1 stated that he was traveling westbound on the 19700 block of Hale Hollow Creek Dr. The driver stated that as he was approaching the intersection, the traffic signal was red. When the light turned green, he continued going forward, attempting to make a right turn onto Mason Road. During the turn, Unit #2 struck the left side of his vehicle. Unit # 1 stated that he did not see Unit # 2 on the crosswalk. Unit # 2 stated she was traveling northbound on the 10700 block of Mason Rd. Crossing the crosswalk with her friend on the electric scooter, as she was passing through attempting to make it to the other side of the sidewalk, she struck Unit # 1 on the left side of the vehicle. Unit # 2 stated that the crosswalk signal was about to turn signaling her to go while she was already on the crosswalk.Witness stated that he was stopped at the red traffic light on Hale Hollow Creek DR attempting to turn left. While he was preparing to proceed forward, he observed Unit #2 continue through the crosswalk and collide with Unit #1. He added that although Unit #1 had the green light, it had not yet entered the intersection when the collision occurred.Unit # 1 showed to have minor front left side damage and it remained operable. Unit # 2 scooter showed not to have any damages. Unit # 2 sustained a minor injury to her right knee and was evaluated by Cy-Fair Emergency Medical Service (EMS) and was not transported. Unit # 1 had no injuries. The parent of the juvenile was on scene and took possession of the scooter.

No injuries were reported, and the vehicle sustained no visible damage. The pedestrian reported that his scooter struck his ankle but confirmed he did not fall or sustain injuries. The scooter sustained damage to the front wheel and left side. No citation was issued, as the incident occurred on private property.

THE DRIVER OF UNIT 1 REPORTED THAT SHE CAME TO A STOP AT THE STOP SIGN WHILE EXITING THE APARTMENT COMPLEX. SHE EXPLAINED THAT SHE BEGAN TO MOVE FORWARD FROM THE STOP SIGN TO MAKE A RIGHT TURN ONTO THE 183A SOUTHBOUND FRONTAGE ROAD WHEN UNIT 2 COLLIDED WITH HER VEHICLE. UNIT 2 WAS TRAVELING NORTHBOUND ON THE SIDEWALK AT THE TIME OF THE IMPACT. THE OPERATOR OF UNIT 2 STATED THAT HE WAS TRAVELING APPROXIMATELY TWENTY MILES PER HOUR AND DID NOT OBSERVE UNIT 1 ENTERING THE CROSSWALK AREA. THE IMPACT FROM UNIT 2 WAS SIGNIFICANT ENOUGH TO CAUSE THE PASSENGER SIDE AIRBAGS IN UNIT 1 TO DEPLOY, WHICH FURTHER SUPPORTS THAT THE SPEED AND FORCE OF UNIT 2'S MOVEMENT WERE SUBSTANTIAL AT THE TIME OF COLLISION.UNDER TEXAS TRANSPORTATION CODE SECTION 552.0035, A SIDEWALK USER MAY NOT APPROACH OR ENTER A CROSSWALK AT A SPEED GREATER THAN IS REASONABLE AND PRUDENT GIVEN EXISTING CONDITIONS AND ANY KNOWN OR POTENTIAL HAZARDS. THE STATUTE FURTHER IDENTIFIES ANY SPEED ABOVE EIGHT MILES PER HOUR AS PRIMA FACIE EVIDENCE THAT THE SPEED IS NOT REASONABLE OR PRUDENT. GIVEN THE SELF REPORTED SPEED OF UNIT 2 AND THE RESULTING COLLISION FORCE, THE EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT UNIT 2 WAS TRAVELING AT A SPEED WELL ABOVE THE STATUTORY THRESHOLD.

crash_records.narrative: The 1998 block of Texas Ave, is a two-lane, two-way roadway used for eastbound and westbound traffic only. The 100 block of South Eucalyptus St, is a two-lane, two-way roadway used for southbound and northbound traffic only. Unit 1 was traveling eastbound on lane number one of 1998 Texas Ave. The pedestrian was riding an electric scooter on lane number one of 1998 Texas Ave. The pedestrian was riding the electric scooter on the wrong side of the roadway. Unit 1 turned right onto the 100 block of south Eucalyptus St, and struck the pedestrian crossing the roadway on his electric scooter. Unit 1 and the pedestrian pulled into the 100 block of south Eucalyptus St. The pedestrian advised his left leg hurt due to the collision but refused medical services at the scene. The driver of unit 1 was cited and released from the scene.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 was traveling eastbound on Wheeler Avenue approaching Rockwood Drive. Upon reaching Rockwood Drive, Unit 1 made a right turn and struck Unit 2, who was riding his electric scooter within the crosswalk and also traveling eastbound crossing Rockwood Drive. The impact caused Unit 2 to be knocked off his scooter, resulting in him falling onto the pavement and hitting his head. Following the collision, Unit 1 left the scene without stopping to render aid or providing their identification information as required by law. Unit 2 sustained bleeding to both legs and a head injury. Unit 2s electric scooter was also damaged and became inoperable. A witness to the incident confirmed that Unit 2 was already within the crosswalk before Unit 1 initiated the right turn. The witness followed Unit 1 after the collision and was able to obtain Unit 1s license plate number, which was later provided to police.

crash_records.narrative: ON 11/15/2025 I RESPONDED TO A CRASH HOTSHOT LOCATED AT THE INTERSECTION OF W. 5TH ST AND RIO GRANDE ST. I ASSISTED AS THE PATROL CRASH INVESTIGATOR WHILE ON SCENE.  I OBSERVED VEHICLE #1 PARKED FACING EASTBOUND AT THE INTERSECTION STATED ABOVE.  I OBSERVED  UNIT #2 IDENTIFIED AS A ELECTRIC SCOOTER. DRIVER OF UNIT #1 WAS ON SCENE. DRIVER OF UNIT #2 WAS NOT ON SCENE WHEN I ARRIVED. DUE TO THE SEVERITY OF INJURIES SUSTAINED; DRIVER OF UNIT #2 WAS IMMEDIATELY TRANSPORTED TO SETON MAIN. DRIVER OF UNIT #1 STATED HE WAS TRAVELING EASTBOUND IN THE MIDDLE LANE. HE SAID A BOX TRUCK  WAS IN FRONT OF HIM SO HE PROCEEDED INTO THE FAR LEFT LANE. AS HE WENT THROUGH THE INTERSECTION ON A GREEN LIGHT, UNIT #2 CRASHED INTO THE FRONT PASSENGER DOOR OF VEHICLE #1. THE IMPACT CAUSED RIGHT SIDE DISTRIBUTED DAMAGE.11/19/25 DETECTIVE SUPPLEMENT: MADE MINIOR CORRECTIONS TO REPORT. UNIT 2 WAS OPERATING AN ELECTRIC SCOOTER AND WAS TRAVELING EASTBOUND IN THE #4 LANE OF W 5TH ST. UNIT 2 MADE A LEFT TURN FROM THE #4 LANE IN AN ATTEMPT TO TRAVEL NB ON RIO GRANDE ST. UNIT 1 WAS TRAVELING EB ON W 5TH ST IN THE #2 LANE AND WAS APPROACHING A RED LIGHT BEHIND A VEHICLE. UNIT 1 THEN CHANGED LANES TO THE #1 LANE AS THE LIGHT BEGAN TO CHANGE. AS THE LIGHT TURNED GREEN UNIT 1 ACCELERATED THROUGH THE INTERSECTION. UNIT 2 THEN COLLIDED WITH THE RIGHT SIDE OF UNIT 1. UNIT 2 WAS TRANSPORTED WITH LIFE THREATENING INJURIES. UNIT 2 SUCCUMBED TO HIS INJURIES AND WAS PRONOUNCED DECEASED THE FOLLOWING DAY.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 and Unit 2 (motor-assisted scooter) were traveling northbound on Roundtrock Trail. Unit 2 was in front of Unit 1. The driver of Unit 1 then started to overtake Unit 2, but the rider of Unit 2 failed to signal their intent to turn and made an unsafe left turn at the Piedra Drive intersection, which caused Unit 2 to collide with Unit 1's RFQ. The rider of Unit 2 was also wearing headphones (driver inattention) and was unaware that Unit 1 was overtaking them. It should be noted that the rider of Unit 2 gave a conflicting statement, and advised they were on the sidewalk. However, Texas Transportation Code 552.0035 states in part that a sidewalk user may not suddenly exit from a curb or other place of safety and proceed into a crosswalk in the path of a vehicle so close that it is impossible for the vehicle operator to stop and yield.

causal_chains.sentence: The driver of Unit 1 then started to overtake Unit 2, but the rider of Unit 2 failed to signal their intent to turn and made an unsafe left turn at the Piedra Drive intersection, which caused Unit 2 to collide with Unit 1's RFQ.

crash_records.narrative: VEHICLE 1 (WHITE HONDA CIVIC) WAS TRAVELING E/B ON FRISCO SQUARE BLVD APPROACHING FRISCO ST.  AFTER STOPPING AT THE STOP SIGN, VEHICLE  1 ATTEMPTED TO MAKE A LEFT-HAND TURN TO GO N/B ONTO FRISCO ST FROM FRISCO SQUARE BLVD AND COLLIDED WITH VEHICLE 2 (TEEWING E-SCOOTER).  AFTER THE COLLISION, VEHICLE 1 CONTINUED TO MAKE THE LEFT TURN ONTO FRISCO ST AND PARKED IN A SPOT NEAR THE CRASH SCENE.  AFTER THE INITIAL COLLISION, THE OPERATOR OF VEHICLE 2, LANDED ON THE SIDEWALK CURB NEXT TO AN SUV, JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION AND HIS E-SCOOTER CAME TO A REST AFTER STRIKING A TREE JUST WEST OF THE INTERSECTION.  THE OPERATOR OF VEHICLE 2 WAS BELIEVED TO BE UNCONSCIOUS AND TRANSPORTED TO THR PLANO HOSPITAL BY FRISCO FD MEDICS FOR FURTHER MEDICAL TREATMENT.  DRIVER 1 DID NOT SUSTAIN INJURIES AND REFUSED MEDICAL TRANSPORT.  VEHICLE 1 SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE TO THE FRONT LEFT BUMPER AREA AND VEHICLE 2 SUSTAINED MODERATE TO HEAVY DAMAGE.  BOTH VEHICLES WERE PLACED ON A HOLD AT L.W. TOWING UNTIL THE INVESTIGATION IS COMPLETED.  A WITNESS ON SCENE ADVISED OFFICERS, SHE WAS SITTING IN HER VEHICLE WITH HER DRIVERSIDE WINDOW DOWN AND THE E-SCOOTER CAME BY HER GOING 25-30MPH AND ZIPPED THROUGH THE INTERSECTION WITHOUT STOPPING FOR THE STOP SIGN.  THE WITNESS ADVISED SHE DID NOT SEE VISIBLE LIGHTS ON VEHICLE 2.  I WAS NOT ABLE TO SPEAK WITH DRIVER 2 DUE TO THE NATURE OF HIS INJURIES BUT WILL SPEAK TO HIM WHEN ABLE, AT WHICH TIME I WILL COMPLETE A SUPPLEMENTAL REPORT IF NEED BE.12.29.2025ON 12.29.2025, I MADE CONTACT WITH DRIVER 2(OMARION JENKINS/E-SCOOTER OPERATOR) IN REFERENCE TO THE CRASH.  OMARION ADVISED HE REMEMBERS CLOCKING OUT FROM WORK (HEB) AT 6:00PM, RIDING HIS SCOOTER  AND THEN WAKING UP IN THE HOSPITAL.  AFTER SPEAKING WITH BOTH PARITES INVOVLVED, IT IS MY OPINION DRIVER 2 DID NOT STOP AT THE STOP SIGN BEFORE PROCEEDING THROUGH THE INTERSECTION AND MY OPINION  IS BASED ON THE FACTS/EVIDENCE I HAVE AVAILABLE TO ME AT THE TIME OF MY INVESTIAGATION.  IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION BECOMES AVAILABLE, IT WILL BE CONSIDERED.  I RESERVE THE RIGHT TO AMEND THIS REPORT IF ADDITIONAL INFORMATION WARRANTS SUCH ACTION.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1 stated she was coming out her alleyway when she came to a complete stop to check S/B traffic on Independence Pkwy. Unit 1 stated she stopped, turned her head to check on traffic and then heard a loud thump from her right side. Unit 2 who was riding an electric scooter on the sidewalk ran into her vehicle. Unit 2 stated he was riding his electric scooter traveling northbound on the S/B sidewalk of Independence Pkwy. He stated he was traveling slow at first and then picked up speed, however, he stated he didn't see Unit 1 when she came out the alleyway. Unit 2 stated he couldn't stop on time and struck Unit 1. Furthermore, he stated his electric scooter doesn't go more than 20 mph, however, he estimated he was going about 10mph.Unit 2 was transported to Medical City Plano.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 1, a black 2021 Ford F-250 TX LP-KSTENO, was traveling northbound on S Powell Pkwy. Unit 2, a elected scooter, was traveling southbound on S Powell Pkwy on the wrong side of the road. As Unit 1 turned eastbound onto Fourth Street it contacted Unit 2. Unit 1 sustained minor front end damage. The rider of Unit 2 stated the scooter sustained rear end damage however nothing significant was observed and the scooter was ridden away from the scene without incident. The rider of Unit 2 refused medical evaluation on scene.

crash_records.narrative: Unit 2 is a self-propelled electric scooter. The scooter was traveling west down Pflugerville Parkway when it approached the intersection of the 130-service road. Unit 2 then entered into the cross walk to continue traveling west. Unit 2 advised he did not know if he pressed the button to engage the pedestrian right of way and he did not recall the color of the light at the intersection for the other traffic continuing west. Unit 1 was also traveling west down Pflugerville Parkway when he turned his vehicle to the right to continue north bound on the 130-service road. When unit 1 turned he struck the electric scooter. Unit 1 pulled over to a safe place with unit 2 and provided necessary documentation.It should be noted that unit 2 advised another individual on a scooter was involved in the collision, but he left the scene and went home. Unable to contact other party.

crash_records.narrative: UNIT 1 WAS TRAVELING EAST ON STAR GAZER WAY. UNIT 2 (JUVENILE) WAS TRAVELING NORTH ON HIDDEN GROVE LANDING ON ELECTRIC SCOOTER. UNIT 1 FAILED TO YROW ENTERING THE INTERSECTION. UNIT 2 IMPACTED THE RFQ OF UNIT 1.

Witnesses stated that U2 was heading south on the 200 block of Losoya St in the #2 lane on an electric scooter. Witnesses claimed to see U1 doing burnouts and revving his engine with another unknown vehicle. After the burnouts, U1 then began weaving in and out of traffic recklessly changing lanes south on the 200 block of Losoya St.  U1 then attempted to pass U2 but failed to do so safely, causing U1's back right to hit U2's left side. The impact caused U2 to fall off of the electric scooter. After the crash, witnesses saw U1 leave the location, eventually stopping down the road to switch drivers and then continue to flee the crime scene. U2 was transported to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. It was later determined that U2 suffered a hip fracture. U1 was a three-wheeled motorcycle vehicle.
